You are the end-of-run deep reviewer for the rust-raw perf-levers run — fresh context, trust nothing. Read napkins/perf-levers/playbook.md in full, then the unit-status table at the top of napkins/perf-levers/run-log.md. Task: review the ENTIRE run delta git diff a103a45..HEAD (a103a45 is the pre-series baseline) against (a) all eight acceptance criteria on backlog task TASK-017 (read via: backlog task view TASK-017 --plain; do NOT edit backlog), (b) the Definition of Done in docs/plans/2026-07-01-003-perf-rust-raw-perf-levers-plan.md, and (c) the durable benchmark log experiments/single-sequencer/orchestration/logs/rust-raw/perf-levers-run-log.md for coherence and honesty as a standalone record. Delegate wide file reading to subagents to protect your context. Also run a remnant sweep: stray temp/WIP files in the tracked tree, leftover experiment code or dead knobs, TODO/FIXME introduced by the series, doc claims contradicting code. For each acceptance criterion state VERIFIED with evidence or a specific gap. Do not re-run benchmarks — the validator owns timing; check claims against the recorded evidence and the tree. Append '## END-OF-RUN DEEP REVIEW' at the very END of napkins/perf-levers/run-log.md (append-only, never insert mid-file) with findings ranked blocking/advisory, then ring exactly once.

When your unit is finished and your run-log DONE/BLOCKED block is written, ring the orchestrator so it does not have to poll (the run-log block is the record — this is only a doorbell):
  /home/grace/Coding/ai-config/bin/herder-send term_655932ec8685e328 'Unit DONE — run-log updated'
Ring exactly ONCE and then stop, whatever it reports. The orchestrator is usually mid-turn when you ring, so the helper will say verify=queued (or even verify=not_delivered) — that is EXPECTED and is NOT a failure: your message is queued and the run-log is the real record. Do NOT resend on a queued/not_delivered result; resending just stacks duplicate messages in the orchestrator's queue.
Do NOT ring with raw 'herdr agent send' — it writes the text without submitting it, so the ring never lands. Use the command above (also available as "$HERDER_SEND" "$HERDER_NOTIFY_TO").

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- ai-config: Use when working in the ai-config repo, editing portable agent skills, changing Claude/Codex/Cursor config, or maintaining ai-setup, ai-doctor, ai-sync, ai-adopt, and ai-push.

Use when the user says "/compound", "compound this", "what did we learn", "capture the learnings from this conversation", or "what's worth keeping from this session". Use when asked to "design an interface", "shape this API", "design it twice", "explore module boundaries", or when planning a new deep module and the first idea is unlikely to be the best. Based on "Design It Twice" from A Philosophy of Software Design — the value is the contrast, not the first draft.

Use when the user says "orchestrate this plan", "run this as a relay", "spawn phase agents", "split this across agents/sessions", "herd this plan", or hands over a substantial implementation plan / long runbook that won't fit one context window. - compound-engineering:ce-test-browser: Run browser tests for pages affected by the current branch or PR.
- compound-engineering:ce-work: Execute a plan or concrete work prompt end-to-end. Use when implementing from docs/plans, a spec path, or a clear build request; use ce-debug for open-ended bugs.

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Review rust-raw perf-levers run against acceptance criteria

content.content:
1	# rust-raw perf-levers run — playbook
2	
3	Shared protocol for executing `docs/plans/2026-07-01-003-perf-rust-raw-perf-levers-plan.md`
4	(the plan — the source of truth for WHAT; this file is HOW). **Every agent reads this file in
5	full, then `napkins/perf-levers/run-log.md`, then only its own unit's plan section** plus the
6	plan's Goal Capsule, Key Technical Decisions, and Verification Contract. Do not read the whole
7	plan or the napkins evidence reports — delegate any wide code reading to subagents where your
8	CLI supports them and keep your own context for the work.
9	
10	## Run shape
11	
12	- Autonomy: autonomous (the owner is away). Sliding-door capture is mandatory — every
13	  could-have-gone-the-other-way decision gets a `SLIDING DOOR` block in the run-log.
14	- Topology: standing orchestrator (fable, the session that wrote this) + strictly sequential
15	  units. Per-unit codex implementer; per-unit reviewer (claude opus for every unit, plus a codex
16	  second reviewer on the load-bearing units); one standing validator (claude on model fable) who
17	  independently re-runs gates.
18	- Liveness: workers idle in their tabs after DONE — keep-open for interrogation until the owner
19	  releases them. The validator stands for the whole run. Nobody culls anybody.
20	- Notify-back address: `term_655932ec8685e328` (the orchestrator's durable terminal id). Spawns
21	  use `herder-spawn --notify`, which injects the exact ring command into your prompt.
22	- Backlog: yes — backlog task TASK-017 is the unit ledger (one acceptance criterion per unit).
23	  **The orchestrator is the sole backlog writer.** Workers never run backlog commands and never
24	  edit files under `backlog/`.
25	- Golden agent: none — the plan and `napkins/perf-levers/orchestration-kickoff.md` carry the
26	  original intent.
27	- Worktree / branch / workspace: `/home/grace/.herdr/worktrees/synfinex/fable-pass`, branch
28	  `fable-pass`, herdr workspace `w655932ec8686525`. **One writer at a time** — a unit's
29	  implementer is the only agent editing the tree while its unit is open; reviewers and the
30	  validator never edit code. The orchestrator writes no implementation code.
31	- Nobody pushes or opens PRs. The owner ships.
32	
33	## Environment facts
34	
35	- All engine/bench commands run from `experiments/single-sequencer/impl/rust-raw`, prefixed
36	  `env -u RUSTFLAGS` (the committed build config: znver4, fat-LTO, cgu=1). Non-interactive
37	  shells may need `source ~/.cargo/env` first.
38	- Benchmark discipline binds: `experiments/single-sequencer/impl/rust-raw/bench/AGENTS.md` plus
39	  the plan's Verification Contract. Alternating A/B ≥3 reps, pinned core, medians, tail-first
40	  (p99.9 never regresses beyond spread), host fingerprint recorded, too-good tripwire.
41	- Pre-series baseline SHA: `a103a45` (docs-only on top of the last engine merge — engine code
42	  identical to `a8c9702`).
43	- perf on this host: use `/usr/lib/linux-tools-6.8.0-124/perf` directly (the wrapper refuses
44	  kernel 6.8.0-94). Generic last-level-cache events are unsupported on this CPU — use the L2
45	  request-stat events plus generic cache-references/misses.
46	- Grid cells: `ENGINE_CORE_MODE=grid ENGINE_CORE_GRID_CELL=<cell> cargo bench --bench
47	  engine_core` for baseline, single-hot-book, many-markets, market-ceiling, mild-shock,
48	  severe-cascade.
49	- QUIET-HOST RULE for timing runs (added 2026-07-02): this host also carries a second
50	  orchestration run (worktree `yamen-hl-replay`). Before any TIMING measurement (cargo bench
51	  runs, A/B reps, microbench floors — not functional tests, not allocation counting), check for
52	  competing heavy processes: `pgrep -af 'cargo (bench|run)|engine_core|hl_stream' | grep -v
53	  <own-worktree>` — if anything heavy is running outside our worktree, wait and retry rather
54	  than measure into contention, and note the wait in your evidence block. Run your own gate
55	  commands sequentially, not concurrently (same target dir — cargo just lock-serializes them).
56	  Owner amendment (2026-07-02): the other run's processes are reniced and do NOT block timing —
57	  record which outside processes were live in your evidence block, keep alternating order-flipped
58	  A/B rounds (background load hits both sides symmetrically), and re-run rather than report any
59	  suspicious result in either direction.
60	- A/B RESOLUTION RULE (added 2026-07-02, from the margin-unit refutation): three reps is the
61	  contract minimum, not a resolution guarantee. Before claiming a ratio, check that per-rep
62	  spread is well under the effect you are claiming; if not, escalate — more alternating rounds
63	  (report the paired win count), and/or the one-million-op measured window
64	  (`ENGINE_CORE_GRID_MEASURED_OPS=1000000`), which cut spread from ±11% to ±1.2% on
65	  many-markets. A ratio whose confidence interval spans 1.0 is not evidence of "flat".
66	- Repo prose rules (root `AGENTS.md`) bind any doc you touch: no opaque identifier schemes in
67	  human prose; documents are current-state snapshots — delete stale text rather than appending
68	  history.
69	
70	## Units — one implementer each. Do ONLY your unit.
71	
72	The plan section named in parentheses is your spec: goal, requirements, files, approach, test
73	scenarios, verification. Units run strictly in this order.
74	
75	1. **Baselines and the margin-core floor microbench** (plan section "U1. Margin-core floor
76	   microbench and pre-series baselines"). Also creates the durable benchmark log
77	   `experiments/single-sequencer/orchestration/logs/rust-raw/perf-levers-run-log.md`
78	   (git-tracked) holding the baseline table, host fingerprint, and the exact repro commands for
79	   the self-baseline response captures; the capture artifacts themselves go under
80	   `napkins/perf-levers/self-baseline/` (gitignored) with their checksums recorded in the
81	   durable log.
82	2. **Shock allocation recycling** (plan section "U2. Shock allocation recycling").
83	3. **The Incremental Margin Aggregate with the rebuilt oracle** (plan section "U3. Incremental
84	   Margin Aggregate with rebuilt oracle"). Load-bearing — double review.
85	4. **Order Record Unification** (plan section "U4. Order Record Unification"). Load-bearing —
86	   double review.
87	5. **Fan-out evidence** (plan section "U5. Fan-out evidence: production-scale shock sizing and
88	   split-mark-batches"). **GATED OUTPUT:** its verdict decides whether the sliced fan-out unit
89	   proceeds. Write the verdict in the run-log and stop — the orchestrator makes the gate call.
90	6. **Batched maker-side effects** (plan section "U6. Batched maker-side effects in liquidation
91	   sweeps").
92	7. **The Sliced Price-Step Fan-out** (plan section "U7. Sliced Price-Step Fan-out").
93	   Load-bearing — double review. Spawned only after the fan-out-evidence gate says proceed.
94	
95	## Protocol — implementer
96	
97	1. Read this file, the run-log, and your unit's plan section (plus Goal Capsule, Key Technical
98	   Decisions, Verification Contract).
99	2. Append `## <unit name> — START` to `napkins/perf-levers/run-log.md`.
100	3. Execute, scoped to your unit only. Capture sliding doors as they happen.
101	4. Gates: the Verification Contract rows that apply to your unit. Run them clean — a cached
102	   green is not evidence. Paste the green lines verbatim into your DONE block. Red you cannot
103	   fix inside your unit's scope → `BLOCKED` block + WIP commit + ring + stop. Never advance
104	   past red.
105	5. Commit on `fable-pass` (no push): conventional style scoped to the area, e.g.
106	   `perf(rust-raw): recycle shock-path allocations (shock allocation recycling unit)`.
107	   Multiple commits are fine; leave the tree clean and record the commit range in the DONE
108	   block.
109	6. Append `## <unit name> — DONE` — files changed, decisions/deviations (each with why),
110	   verification evidence verbatim, A/B tables where the unit makes a perf claim.
111	7. Ring the orchestrator exactly once with the injected command. `verify=queued` is success —
112	   never resend. Then idle in your tab; review findings may come back to you for remediation
113	   (remediate, commit, append to your DONE block, ring once).
114	
115	## Protocol — reviewer
116	
117	Read this file, the unit's DONE block in the run-log, the unit's plan section, and the commit
118	range's diff. Review for: correctness against the Key Technical Decisions, the
119	behavior-preservation constraint (byte-identical responses for the behavior-preserving units),
120	test scenarios from the plan actually present, no scope creep, no abandoned experiment code.
121	You edit nothing. Append `## <unit name> — REVIEW (claude|codex)` with findings ranked blocking
122	/ advisory, each with file:line evidence. Ring once, idle. On remediation you re-review only
123	the remediation commits.
124	
125	## Protocol — validator (standing, model fable)
126	
127	You own independent verification — the run's "trust nothing" instance. When the orchestrator
128	rings you with a unit name: re-run that unit's pinned Verification Contract gates from a clean
129	state (`cargo clean -p engine -p bench` first when a cached green is possible; never edit the
130	tree), run the self-baseline response diff where the unit claims behavior preservation, and
131	spot-check one or two of the DONE block's claims against reality. Append
132	`## <unit name> — VALIDATED` with pasted evidence, or `## <unit name> — REFUTED` with the
133	failing output. Refuse unsupported claims plainly: if you cannot reproduce an assertion, say
134	so — do not soften it. Never edit code or backlog. You live for the whole run: keep context
135	lean, delegate reading to subagents, and if your context balloons, `herder-send-self /compact`
136	steered on "validator role, gates per unit, evidence so far".
137	
138	## Decisions already made — do not re-litigate
139	
140	- Owner rulings (2026-07-01, binding): the cross-impl golden fixtures and the wire-format
141	  freeze are retired; rust-raw is the source of what matters; behavior-preserving levers gate
142	  on self-baseline response diffs against our own pre-series build; the sliced fan-out may
143	  change response interleaving.
144	- Cached margin contributions are summed at read time in ascending market order — no f64
145	  running totals (non-associative drift across the ±$1 guard band changes decisions).
146	- One market-inputs epoch per market, bumped by mark writes and market-config overwrites;

# rust-raw perf-levers run — playbook

Shared protocol for executing `docs/plans/2026-07-01-003-perf-rust-raw-perf-levers-plan.md`
(the plan — the source of truth for WHAT; this file is HOW). **Every agent reads this file in
full, then `napkins/perf-levers/run-log.md`, then only its own unit's plan section** plus the
plan's Goal Capsule, Key Technical Decisions, and Verification Contract. Do not read the whole
plan or the napkins evidence reports — delegate any wide code reading to subagents where your
CLI supports them and keep your own context for the work.

## Run shape

- Autonomy: autonomous (the owner is away). Sliding-door capture is mandatory — every
  could-have-gone-the-other-way decision gets a `SLIDING DOOR` block in the run-log.
- Topology: standing orchestrator (fable, the session that wrote this) + strictly sequential
  units. Per-unit codex implementer; per-unit reviewer (claude opus for every unit, plus a codex
  second reviewer on the load-bearing units); one standing validator (claude on model fable) who
  independently re-runs gates.
- Liveness: workers idle in their tabs after DONE — keep-open for interrogation until the owner
  releases them. The validator stands for the whole run. Nobody culls anybody.
- Notify-back address: `term_655932ec8685e328` (the orchestrator's durable terminal id). Spawns
  use `herder-spawn --notify`, which injects the exact ring command into your prompt.
- Backlog: yes — backlog task TASK-017 is the unit ledger (one acceptance criterion per unit).
  **The orchestrator is the sole backlog writer.** Workers never run backlog commands and never
  edit files under `backlog/`.
- Golden agent: none — the plan and `napkins/perf-levers/orchestration-kickoff.md` carry the
  original intent.
- Worktree / branch / workspace: `/home/grace/.herdr/worktrees/synfinex/fable-pass`, branch
  `fable-pass`, herdr workspace `w655932ec8686525`. **One writer at a time** — a unit's
  implementer is the only agent editing the tree while its unit is open; reviewers and the
  validator never edit code. The orchestrator writes no implementation code.
- Nobody pushes or opens PRs. The owner ships.

## Environment facts

- All engine/bench commands run from `experiments/single-sequencer/impl/rust-raw`, prefixed
  `env -u RUSTFLAGS` (the committed build config: znver4, fat-LTO, cgu=1). Non-interactive
  shells may need `source ~/.cargo/env` first.
- Benchmark discipline binds: `experiments/single-sequencer/impl/rust-raw/bench/AGENTS.md` plus
  the plan's Verification Contract. Alternating A/B ≥3 reps, pinned core, medians, tail-first
  (p99.9 never regresses beyond spread), host fingerprint recorded, too-good tripwire.
- Pre-series baseline SHA: `a103a45` (docs-only on top of the last engine merge — engine code
  identical to `a8c9702`).
- perf on this host: use `/usr/lib/linux-tools-6.8.0-124/perf` directly (the wrapper refuses
  kernel 6.8.0-94). Generic last-level-cache events are unsupported on this CPU — use the L2
  request-stat events plus generic cache-references/misses.
- Grid cells: `ENGINE_CORE_MODE=grid ENGINE_CORE_GRID_CELL=<cell> cargo bench --bench
  engine_core` for baseline, single-hot-book, many-markets, market-ceiling, mild-shock,
  severe-cascade.
- QUIET-HOST RULE for timing runs (added 2026-07-02): this host also carries a second
  orchestration run (worktree `yamen-hl-replay`). Before any TIMING measurement (cargo bench
  runs, A/B reps, microbench floors — not functional tests, not allocation counting), check for
  competing heavy processes: `pgrep -af 'cargo (bench|run)|engine_core|hl_stream' | grep -v
  <own-worktree>` — if anything heavy is running outside our worktree, wait and retry rather
  than measure into contention, and note the wait in your evidence block. Run your own gate
  commands sequentially, not concurrently (same target dir — cargo just lock-serializes them).
  Owner amendment (2026-07-02): the other run's processes are reniced and do NOT block timing —
  record which outside processes were live in your evidence block, keep alternating order-flipped
  A/B rounds (background load hits both sides symmetrically), and re-run rather than report any
  suspicious result in either direction.
- A/B RESOLUTION RULE (added 2026-07-02, from the margin-unit refutation): three reps is the
  contract minimum, not a resolution guarantee. Before claiming a ratio, check that per-rep
  spread is well under the effect you are claiming; if not, escalate — more alternating rounds
  (report the paired win count), and/or the one-million-op measured window
  (`ENGINE_CORE_GRID_MEASURED_OPS=1000000`), which cut spread from ±11% to ±1.2% on
  many-markets. A ratio whose confidence interval spans 1.0 is not evidence of "flat".
- Repo prose rules (root `AGENTS.md`) bind any doc you touch: no opaque identifier schemes in
  human prose; documents are current-state snapshots — delete stale text rather than appending
  history.

## Units — one implementer each. Do ONLY your unit.

The plan section named in parentheses is your spec: goal, requirements, files, approach, test
scenarios, verification. Units run strictly in this order.

1. **Baselines and the margin-core floor microbench** (plan section "U1. Margin-core floor
   microbench and pre-series baselines"). Also creates the durable benchmark log
   `experiments/single-sequencer/orchestration/logs/rust-raw/perf-levers-run-log.md`
   (git-tracked) holding the baseline table, host fingerprint, and the exact repro commands for
   the self-baseline response captures; the capture artifacts themselves go under
   `napkins/perf-levers/self-baseline/` (gitignored) with their checksums recorded in the
   durable log.
2. **Shock allocation recycling** (plan section "U2. Shock allocation recycling").
3. **The Incremental Margin Aggregate with the rebuilt oracle** (plan section "U3. Incremental
   Margin Aggregate with rebuilt oracle"). Load-bearing — double review.
4. **Order Record Unification** (plan section "U4. Order Record Unification"). Load-bearing —
   double review.
5. **Fan-out evidence** (plan section "U5. Fan-out evidence: production-scale shock sizing and
   split-mark-batches"). **GATED OUTPUT:** its verdict decides whether the sliced fan-out unit
   proceeds. Write the verdict in the run-log and stop — the orchestrator makes the gate call.
6. **Batched maker-side effects** (plan section "U6. Batched maker-side effects in liquidation
   sweeps").
7. **The Sliced Price-Step Fan-out** (plan section "U7. Sliced Price-Step Fan-out").
   Load-bearing — double review. Spawned only after the fan-out-evidence gate says proceed.

## Protocol — implementer

1. Read this file, the run-log, and your unit's plan section (plus Goal Capsule, Key Technical
   Decisions, Verification Contract).
2. Append `## <unit name> — START` to `napkins/perf-levers/run-log.md`.
3. Execute, scoped to your unit only. Capture sliding doors as they happen.
4. Gates: the Verification Contract rows that apply to your unit. Run them clean — a cached
   green is not evidence. Paste the green lines verbatim into your DONE block. Red you cannot
   fix inside your unit's scope → `BLOCKED` block + WIP commit + ring + stop. Never advance
   past red.
5. Commit on `fable-pass` (no push): conventional style scoped to the area, e.g.
   `perf(rust-raw): recycle shock-path allocations (shock allocation recycling unit)`.
   Multiple commits are fine; leave the tree clean and record the commit range in the DONE
   block.
6. Append `## <unit name> — DONE` — files changed, decisions/deviations (each with why),
   verification evidence verbatim, A/B tables where the unit makes a perf claim.
7. Ring the orchestrator exactly once with the injected command. `verify=queued` is success —
   never resend. Then idle in your tab; review findings may come back to you for remediation
   (remediate, commit, append to your DONE block, ring once).

## Protocol — reviewer

Read this file, the unit's DONE block in the run-log, the unit's plan section, and the commit
range's diff. Review for: correctness against the Key Technical Decisions, the
behavior-preservation constraint (byte-identical responses for the behavior-preserving units),
test scenarios from the plan actually present, no scope creep, no abandoned experiment code.
You edit nothing. Append `## <unit name> — REVIEW (claude|codex)` with findings ranked blocking
/ advisory, each with file:line evidence. Ring once, idle. On remediation you re-review only
the remediation commits.

## Protocol — validator (standing, model fable)

You own independent verification — the run's "trust nothing" instance. When the orchestrator
rings you with a unit name: re-run that unit's pinned Verification Contract gates from a clean
state (`cargo clean -p engine -p bench` first when a cached green is possible; never edit the
tree), run the self-baseline response diff where the unit claims behavior preservation, and
spot-check one or two of the DONE block's claims against reality. Append
`## <unit name> — VALIDATED` with pasted evidence, or `## <unit name> — REFUTED` with the
failing output. Refuse unsupported claims plainly: if you cannot reproduce an assertion, say
so — do not soften it. Never edit code or backlog. You live for the whole run: keep context
lean, delegate reading to subagents, and if your context balloons, `herder-send-self /compact`
steered on "validator role, gates per unit, evidence so far".

## Decisions already made — do not re-litigate

- Owner rulings (2026-07-01, binding): the cross-impl golden fixtures and the wire-format
  freeze are retired; rust-raw is the source of what matters; behavior-preserving levers gate
  on self-baseline response diffs against our own pre-series build; the sliced fan-out may
  change response interleaving.
- Cached margin contributions are summed at read time in ascending market order — no f64
  running totals (non-associative drift across the ±$1 guard band changes decisions).
- One market-inputs epoch per market, bumped by mark writes and market-config overwrites;
  own-account events update cells eagerly at the state-mutation sites (`fold_position`,
  register/deregister resting order, the leverage handler), not in per-command handlers.
- The rebuilt oracle's invariant is refresh-then-compare at end of fold; epoch-current entries
  must already be bit-equal before refresh.
- The hypothetical order stays an overlay; transient default accounts never create cache
  entries; a reduce-only hypothetical does not add its market to the touched set.
- Unification dedupes identity, not lifecycle: engine-only accumulators stay engine-side (the
  matcher frees filled slab nodes before `submit` returns); owner+market stay in the table
  entry; slab reclaim timing is untouched — a design that needs it changed stops and surfaces.
- The slice budget is an engine-construction parameter; unbounded reproduces today's behavior
  (kept for A/B and fallback); the slice atom is one candidate's full enforcement; advancement
  hooks fold, not accept; second-batch coalescing is depth-one; the cursor is real cloned,
  memory-reported engine state — never scratch (`Engine::clone` deliberately empties scratch).
- The fills buffer becomes one engine-level pool owned via `OrderOutcome`; all three consumers
  return it.
- Acceptance is floor-plus-target with the attribution escape; the too-good tripwire applies in
  reverse (a suspiciously cheap cached admission means a missed refresh trigger, not a win).

## Escalate (stop, don't improvise) on

- A behavior-preserving unit's self-baseline diff shows a genuine decision change rather than a
  fixable bug.
- The fan-out-evidence verdict says slicing is unnecessary (Goal Capsule stop condition).
- A target missed while the post-change profile shows the targeted cost source removed — record
  the honest miss, stop chasing.
- Any design pressure to change slab reclaim timing, the journal/replay contract beyond the
  plan's sliced-fan-out shape, or anything outside your unit.
- Anything requiring a push, a PR, culling another agent, or an owner-only decision.

## Context discipline

Own ONE unit. If it balloons: WIP commit + `HANDOFF (continue)` block in the run-log, then
either compact in place (claude agents: `herder-send-self /compact <steer>`) or stop and let
the orchestrator spawn a fresh copy against your HANDOFF block. The orchestrator self-compacts
at unit boundaries when its context approaches 200k.

Task TASK-017 - Execute the rust-raw perf-levers plan (margin cache, order footprint, shock fan-out)
==================================================

Status: ◒ In Progress
Ordinal: 21000
Assignee: @fable
Created: 2026-07-01 22:38
Updated: 2026-07-02 10:48

Description:
--------------------------------------------------
Execute docs/plans/2026-07-01-003-perf-rust-raw-perf-levers-plan.md autonomously (orchestrated multi-agent run). The plan implements the four levers from doc-007 in experiments/single-sequencer/impl/rust-raw. Owner ruling 2026-07-01: cross-impl golden fixtures and the wire-format freeze are retired — rust-raw is the source of what matters; behavior-preserving levers are verified by self-baseline response diffs; the sliced fan-out may change response interleaving.

Acceptance Criteria:
--------------------------------------------------
- [x] #1 Margin-core floor microbench built and pre-series baselines captured (grid rows, response-stream captures, baseline SHA)
- [x] #2 Shock allocation recycling landed: mild-shock bytes/op at or below ~1,000, self-baseline diff clean
- [x] #3 Order Record Unification landed: measured per-order footprint at or below ~220 B with no hot-path hop regressions
- [x] #4 Fan-out evidence recorded: production-scale shock sizing cell and split-mark-batches results gate the slicing work
- [x] #5 Sliced Price-Step Fan-out landed behind a construction-time slice budget with documented semantics, sliced-vs-unsliced evidence, and a chosen default
- [x] #6 Stale docs corrected per the plan's documentation requirement; no abandoned experiment code; run-log carries final benchmark tables with host fingerprint
- [x] #7 Incremental Margin Aggregate executed to its validated conclusion: lever measured net-negative on the contracted A/B (many-markets 0.88x median; both shock cells regressed including p99.9) and reverted with tree-equality proof; evidence and reopening condition recorded in the durable benchmark log
- [x] #8 Batched maker-side effects executed to its validated conclusion: lever measured net-negative on the contracted metrics (fair-test review confirmed faithful implementation with no removable overhead; ten-round sign-check: severe-cascade worst stall 1.198x, mild-shock 1.100x, throughput down both cells, no shock cell wins) and reverted with tree-equality proof; mechanism and reopening condition recorded in the durable benchmark log

Definition of Done:
--------------------------------------------------
No Definition of Done items defined

Implementation Plan:
--------------------------------------------------
1. Orchestrated per napkins/perf-levers/playbook.md: autonomous, strictly sequential units; codex implementers, claude opus review on every unit plus codex second review on the load-bearing units (margin aggregate, unification, sliced fan-out), standing fable validator independently re-runs gates.
2. Unit order: baselines + margin-core floor microbench -> shock allocation recycling -> Incremental Margin Aggregate with rebuilt oracle -> Order Record Unification -> fan-out evidence (gates the sliced fan-out) -> batched maker-side effects -> Sliced Price-Step Fan-out.
3. Evidence ledger: napkins/perf-levers/run-log.md (branch-local); durable benchmark tables: experiments/single-sequencer/orchestration/logs/rust-raw/perf-levers-run-log.md (committed, created by the baselines unit).
4. Gates per the plan's Verification Contract; behavior-preserving units diff responses against the a103a45 self-baseline captures; every perf claim follows the A/B discipline.

Implementation Notes:
--------------------------------------------------
Baselines unit complete and validated (commits 335ff3d + c639e30). Margin-core floor microbench landed as ENGINE_CORE_MODE=margin-core; pre-series baseline tables + host fingerprint + capture checksums committed in experiments/single-sequencer/orchestration/logs/rust-raw/perf-levers-run-log.md; self-baseline response captures under napkins/perf-levers/self-baseline/ verified deterministic (byte-identical repetitions). Review found the capture clean-tree guard bench-scoped and not wired to the capture paths (2 blocking + 3 advisory findings); all five remediated and re-review cleared. Validator re-ran gates from a scratch build: floor stable at 0.53% spread, within ~1.2% of committed baseline.

Shock allocation recycling complete and validated (commits 5d8caa6 + 43362a0). Engine-level fills pool via OrderOutcome, price-batch decode-into scratch, reusable changed-market/account/sorted-position scratch. Review empirically decomposed the initial 22,858-to-409 bytes/op headline and found ~99% was a same-commit rider methodology change; ruling made the CountingSink+warmed rider the canonical engine-allocation ruler and the honest result is mild-shock 4,799-to-409 bytes/op (~12x, under the 1,000 target), reproduced exactly by the validator. A trailing-byte price-batch decode divergence was caught and exact parity restored (pinned by test; tightening recorded as owner follow-up). All 12 self-baseline response streams byte-identical. Engine suite now 156 tests.

Incremental Margin Aggregate closed as measured-net-negative and REVERTED (revert 1d72b5a, tree-equal to c2c8863 by proof; durable-log closeout ab65d5e; revert validated). Full cycle: initial e51d9a1 had a pathological all-account debug oracle (caught live: 25-min bench gate vs 5.5s norm); double review found four blocking defects (maintained f64 running total violating ascending read-time summation; oracle rebuilding before comparing — structurally blind to the drift; shock health gate never routed through the cache; unprotocoled perf evidence). Remediation c44fc1c fixed all correctness items (validated: 166 tests, oracle-on release pass, 12/12 byte-identical streams) but the standing validator refuted the perf claim: contracted A/B shows many-markets 0.88x median — the implementer's 1.005x was a 3-rep artifact inside ±11% spread (A/B RESOLUTION RULE added to playbook) — with counters +18% instructions / +32% L2 requests per op, and both shock cells regressed on every metric including p99.9 (severe-cascade 0.667x, p99.9 +35%): a tail-first gate violation. Ruling: revert; gating rejected (~1000 lines, no measured win anywhere). One bright spot recorded (many-markets tails −21–28%) plus reopening condition in the durable log. Downstream: sliced fan-out detection-cost assumptions must be re-weighed — the health gate remains a full fold per candidate.

Order Record Unification complete and VALIDATED @2e4cfbc (durable-log closeout @9d646be). Footprint machine-checked at 220.01 B/order (mem-report test asserts <=220.1; was ~321). Behavior preserved: 12/12 self-baseline streams byte-identical; all gates green from clean state. Perf at the A/B resolution-rule standard: many-markets 1.131x median throughput on the validator's six order-flipped 1M-op rounds (6/6 paired wins on every metric, non-overlapping ranges; implementer's three-rep 1.203x was window variance around the same win), tails improved (p99.9 0.967x); market-ceiling 1.087x over eleven implementer reps (p99.9 0.854x); single-hot-book flat within noise (0.999x) — the added id-resolved index hop costs nothing measurable where cache was already resident, satisfying the no-hot-path-hop-regression wording at measured level. Double review cleared with zero blocking findings (first unit in the run to skip remediation); claude advisories on hop-count wording and the cum_notional i128->i64 narrowing (safe under the documented arithmetic budget) accepted as record.

Fan-out evidence complete and VALIDATED @84a0ece+5275be3+c563d1f (durable-log entry @123fc15). Scale-gated fanout-evidence cell (1M accounts, 100 markets, exposure-holder + split-mark-batch knobs) landed with fail-loud truncation guard and byte-identity pins. Sizing evidence at the resolution-rule standard, order-flipped rounds under the owner's reniced-host ruling: unsliced worst price-step stall 17.7ms @10k holders, 76.4ms @50k, 135.7ms @100k vs ~100us step budget — three orders of magnitude over, growing with holder count; split-four cuts breadth ~4x (3/3 paired wins, throughput flat) but still leaves ~35ms @100k. Review independently recomputed every number from raw logs (exact match); validator reproduced the 100k point (139.4ms/40.9ms, same regime). GATE RULING: sliced fan-out gate OPEN — splitting is a breadth reducer, not a substitute for resumable slicing; riders recorded (stall columns are the acceptance metric, never whole-row percentiles; detection cost weighed at full-fold-per-candidate; split-batches shipping default is a unit-7 A/B question).

Batched maker-side effects closed as measured-net-negative and REVERTED (revert 4e20f7e, tree-equal to bd1ab2a at the tree-object level; durable-log closeout a8161aa; revert validated: engine 158 + bench 54 green from clean, no batching symbols remain). Full cycle: implementer landed a correct, byte-identical implementation (12/12 streams) but BLOCKED on its own gate — severe-cascade stall regressed 3/3 rounds across three design shapes. Fair-test review: faithful to plan U6, no removable overhead (pooled batches, index sort, coalescing does fewer lookups), regression in the lever's best-case cell. Validator sign-check escalated 2 to 10 rounds/cell per the A/B resolution rule: worst stall 1.198x severe / 1.100x mild, throughput 0.94x/0.93x, 7-9/10 paired losses, NO shock cell wins. Mechanism: order unification already made maker order-table writes dense/cache-friendly, so the deferred store/load round-trip + mandated sort are pure added cost. Reopening: only if the sliced fan-out re-derives the need across slice boundaries under its own A/B. AC renumbering: sliced fan-out=#5, docs sweep=#6, margin closure=#7, this closure=#8.

Sliced Price-Step Fan-out complete (commits 40d4da9 + 42ab47b + durable log 8f719bc). Construction-time PriceHealthSliceBudget with unbounded default: all twelve self-baseline streams byte-identical at HEAD (re-proven after remediation). Finite-budget-one at the million-account fanout-evidence cell: worst price-batch stall 128.9ms -> 3.77ms (34.2x, validator-reproduced 2/2 order-flipped rounds), mean 471x, judged on the direct stall columns per the gate ruling. Double review zero blocking; accepted advisories recorded in the durable log: the stall is shrunk ~32x and still grows with changed-market holder count under the current snapshot design (snapshot-gather slicing is the reopening condition); the cell throughput column is op-count-mix inflated by bench drain folds (honest whole-run figure: perf elapsed ratio ~1.34x). Remediation hardened the replay contract: the slice budget setter refuses once sequencing has begun or price-health work is pending, and the bench applies budgets at fixture construction. Cursor is cloned engine state pinned by clone-parity and deterministic-replay tests; duplicate/malformed inputs never advance; second batches coalesce depth-one.

Final docs sweep complete (commits 032175b + e6c215c). Stale docs corrected as current-state snapshots: retired frozen-stream/margin-cache-oracle gate language replaced by the self-baseline capture rule; reverted levers documented as tried-and-bounded, not assumed; fanout-evidence throughput column documented as op-count-mix with the direct stall columns as the gate; bench self-report strings retitled truthfully. Abandoned-code sweep across ~30 symbols from both reverted levers found nothing; every remaining hit verified intentional by review. Series-final HEAD benchmark snapshot appended to the durable log (host fingerprint, repro commands, three zero-reject full-grid reps, final fanout pair 32.8x worst / 437.7x mean). Review: zero findings, every durable-log ratio recomputed. Validator: sources unchanged except the descriptive strings, suites green from clean, independent grid rep matches allocs/bytes exactly with timing inside single-rep spread; its one advisory (evidence-pointer sentence not covering the two new bullets) remediated in e6c215c.

content.text:
I've read the full plan. Here is the verbatim extraction you requested.

---

## 1. Definition of Done (exact text, every item)

The plan has a dedicated `## Definition of Done` section (lines 233-243). Verbatim:

- "Every implementation unit landed with its gates green, or explicitly stopped with evidence per the Goal Capsule stop conditions, recorded in the run-log."
- "Behavior preservation: the self-baseline response diff is clean for every behavior-preserving unit, and U7's unbounded-budget config reproduces it."
- "Targets: many-markets ≥1.4× pre-series baseline (same-host A/B medians) and mild-shock ≤1,000 bytes/op — or the attribution escape documented (targeted cost source demonstrably removed, honest miss recorded)."
- "Floor: no standard grid cell's p99.9 regressed beyond measurement spread vs the U1 baseline."
- "The rebuilt margin oracle runs silent across the full suite and grid in debug (plus one release-with-debug-assertions pass)."
- "Sliced fan-out: machinery landed behind the slice budget; new semantics documented in the engine docs; sliced-vs-unsliced evidence table and the chosen default budget in the run-log."
- "Stale docs corrected (R10 list), including the fills-buffer do-not-readopt note and the performance doc's gate description."
- "No abandoned experiment code in the final diff; napkins remain untouched by this plan (they harvest at branch end)."
- "Backlog: an execution task tracks this plan; per-unit notes and the final summary recorded via the backlog CLI."

### Goal Capsule success criteria (the "Acceptance" decision, line 84) — floor-plus-target framing

The plan also encodes success criteria under Key Technical Decisions as **"Acceptance is floor-plus-target with an attribution escape."** Verbatim:

> "Floor (must hold): no standard cell's p99.9 regresses more than measurement spread, all in-tree gates green, self-baseline response diff clean for behavior-preserving levers. Targets: many-markets throughput ≥1.4× the pre-series baseline (same-host alternating A/B medians) and mild-shock bytes/op ≤1,000. If a target is missed but the post-change profile shows the targeted cost source removed (margin math no longer dominant; allocation sites gone), record the honest result and stop rather than chase — the 'too-good number means nothing happened' tripwire applies in reverse too: a suspiciously cheap cached admission means a missed refresh trigger, not a win."

---

## 2. Verification Contract (every gate/row, exact numerics)

Header (line 216): "All engine/bench commands run from `experiments/single-sequencer/impl/rust-raw` with the committed build config: prefix `env -u RUSTFLAGS` (CI does not exist; these gates are convention and this plan's contract)."

The gate table (verbatim, Gate | Command | Applies to):

- **Engine lib suite (152+ tests)** | `cargo test --lib -p engine` | every unit
- **Engine boundary integration** | `cargo test --test boundary_live -p engine` | U2, U3, U4, U7
- **Bench suite incl. grid gates** | `cargo test -p bench` and `cargo test -p bench -- --ignored` | every unit touching bench; U2/U3/U4 for grid gates
- **Grid cells, zero rejects** | `ENGINE_CORE_MODE=grid ENGINE_CORE_GRID_CELL=<cell> cargo bench --bench engine_core` for baseline, single-hot-book, many-markets, market-ceiling, mild-shock, severe-cascade | U2–U7
- **Self-baseline response diff** | diff responses of fixed-seed streams against the U1 pre-series capture (`.obox` via `SPINE_OBOX_PATH` or bench sink capture) | U2, U3, U4, U6, and U7 at unbounded budget
- **Margin oracle** | debug-assertion runs of the lib suite + grid cells (plus one release-with-debug-assertions pass) | U3 onward
- **A/B benchmark protocol** | alternating old/new binaries ≥3 reps, pinned core, medians, vs the U1 baseline SHA; host fingerprint recorded | every perf claim
- **Profile attribution** | `/usr/lib/linux-tools-6.8.0-124/perf` (wrapper broken for this kernel; generic LLC events unsupported — use L2 request-stats + cache-references) | U3, U7 verdicts

**Quality gates row (line 229), verbatim:** "tail-first (p99.9 holds or improves on every standard cell — never trade tail for p50); allocs/op reported, never gated except U2's explicit target; 'too-good' tripwire (an implausibly cheap result triggers a missed-work audit before celebration)."

Related discipline (R9, line 54): "committed build config (`env -u RUSTFLAGS`, znver4/fat-LTO/cgu=1), same-host alternating A/B (≥3 reps, medians, pinned core), tail-gated (p99.9 must hold or improve on every standard cell), host fingerprint recorded."

**Numeric thresholds appearing in / bound to the Verification Contract:**
- Engine lib suite: **152+ tests**
- A/B: **≥3 reps**, medians, pinned core
- many-markets throughput: **≥1.4×** pre-series baseline
- mild-shock bytes/op: **≤1,000** (U2's explicit gated target)
- p99.9: holds or improves on every standard cell (tail-first, never trade tail for p50)
- Six standard grid cells named: baseline, single-hot-book, many-markets, market-ceiling, mild-shock, severe-cascade

---

## 3. The Four Levers — land vs. abandon expectation, and thresholds

The four committed levers (Goal Capsule, line 17): "the Incremental Margin Aggregate, shock allocation recycling, Order Record Unification, and the Sliced Price-Step Fan-out with batched maker effects."

Note on mapping to units: the four "levers" span the implementation units. Behavior-preserving group = margin cache, unification, recycling, batched effects (R3). Below I give each lever with its expectation and explicit thresholds.

**Lever A — Incremental Margin Aggregate (U3; requirements R1, R2, R3, R10)**
- Expected to land (the "dominant lever," line 137). Behavior-preserving: must produce byte-identical responses (R3).
- Explicit success threshold: many-markets throughput **≥1.4×** the pre-series baseline (same-host alternating A/B medians). Verification (line 169): "many-markets A/B vs U1 baseline shows the margin path's profile share collapsed ... and throughput ≥1.4× floor-tracked; microbench floor for near-margin admissions drops materially; oracle silent across all runs."
- Abandonment/attribution escape (line 84): if the ≥1.4× target is missed but the profile shows "margin math no longer dominant," record the honest result and stop rather than chase. Stop condition (c) (line 19): "the many-markets floor target is missed after the margin-cache profile shows the targeted cost removed (report honestly, do not chase)."
- Reverse tripwire: "a suspiciously cheap cached admission means a missed refresh trigger, not a win."

**Lever B — Shock allocation recycling (U2; requirements R4, R3, R10)**
- Expected to land (the "quick win," line 137). Byte-identical (R3).
- Explicit success threshold: R4 (line 45) "Target: mild-shock bytes/op from ~22,900 to under ~1,000." U2 Verification (line 158): "mild-shock bytes/op **≤1,000** on this host; no standard cell p99.9 regression beyond spread (alternating A/B vs U1 baseline); behavior preservation per R3." Test scenario (line 157): "allocs/op drops by an order of magnitude."
- This is the only allocs/op-gated target (per Quality gates line 229: "never gated except U2's explicit target").

**Lever C — Order Record Unification (U4; requirements R7, R3)**
- Expected to land ("footprint," line 137). Byte-identical (R3).
- Explicit threshold: R7 (line 50) "The per-order resident footprint drops from ~321 B/order toward ~200 B/order." U4 test scenario (line 178): "footprint assertion: measured bytes/order across the four structures **≤ ~220 B** in the mem-report view for a seeded book"; also "cancel-by-cloid hop count not increased." Verification (line 179): "grid market-ceiling and many-markets A/B report improved or unchanged throughput and p50 with no tail regression; mem-report table in the run-log shows the footprint drop."
- Note the two figures: R7 target directionally "~321 B/order toward ~200 B/order"; the test-scenario hard assertion is **≤ ~220 B**.

**Lever D — Sliced Price-Step Fan-out with batched maker effects (U7, with U5 evidence gate and U6 batched effects; requirements R5, R3, R8; R6 for batched effects)**
- This lever is conditional / may be gated out. It is "the journal-shaping commitment" (line 203) and deliberately changes response interleaving (permitted).
- **U5 is the evidence gate that decides whether the slicing machinery proceeds.** Verification (line 189): "This unit's output gates U7: proceed when the unsliced stall grows superlinearly-or-linearly with holders and materially exceeds the ~100 µs step budget at scale (expected from the 2.2 ms / 10k-account measurement); otherwise stop and surface." Stop condition (b) (line 19): "the fan-out evidence unit shows slicing is unnecessary."
- Explicit numeric anchors in the fan-out chain: "~100 µs step budget" (U5, line 189); "2.2 ms / 10k-account measurement" (line 189; also stated line 33 as "up to ~2.2 ms at only 10k accounts"); shock baseline "~73k ops/s with ~22.9 KB allocated per op" (line 33).
- U7 success/floor thresholds (line 210): "sliced-mode run of the U5 scale cell shows interleaved order-flow p99.9 improved by the slicing (record the factor honestly — **target ≥5×** on the worst-step-stall metric at the U5 scale, **floor: any material improvement with zero correctness findings**)." Also: "unbounded-budget config passes every gate in the Verification Contract unchanged."
- Fallback/retention: the unbounded (unsliced) mode is retained for A/B measurement and as fallback if slicing misbehaves (R5 line 46; decision line 81). The shipped default budget is chosen by the sliced-fan-out unit's evidence.
- **U6 Batched maker-side effects (R6, R3):** expected to land, byte-identical ("byte-identically," line 48/191). Verification (line 199): "severe-cascade A/B tail (p99/p99.9) holds or improves; no regression on generator cells." No standalone numeric target beyond tail-hold.

---

## 4. Stop conditions / escalation triggers (verbatim)

**Goal Capsule "Stop conditions" (line 19), verbatim:**
> "Stop and surface to the owner if (a) a behavior-preserving lever changes responses on the self-baseline diff and the cause is a genuine decision change rather than a bug; (b) the fan-out evidence unit shows slicing is unnecessary; (c) the many-markets floor target is missed after the margin-cache profile shows the targeted cost removed (report honestly, do not chase)."

**Attribution escape / "stop rather than chase" (Acceptance decision, line 84):**
> "If a target is missed but the post-change profile shows the targeted cost source removed (margin math no longer dominant; allocation sites gone), record the honest result and stop rather than chase — the 'too-good number means nothing happened' tripwire applies in reverse too: a suspiciously cheap cached admission means a missed refresh trigger, not a win."

**U5 → U7 gate / escalation (line 189):**
> "This unit's output gates U7: proceed when the unsliced stall grows superlinearly-or-linearly with holders and materially exceeds the ~100 µs step budget at scale (expected from the 2.2 ms / 10k-account measurement); otherwise stop and surface."

**U4 slab-reclaim escalation (line 177):**
> "Do not change slab reclaim timing in this unit ... if the design genuinely needs it, stop and surface per the Goal Capsule."

**Too-good tripwire (Quality gates, line 229):** "'too-good' tripwire (an implausibly cheap result triggers a missed-work audit before celebration)."

---

### Additional numeric anchors worth flagging for a deep review (context figures, not gates)

From the Problem Frame (line 33) and requirements — baseline "before" numbers the targets are measured against:
- Generator throughput: "2.34M ops/s at 10k accounts × 100 markets down to 538k at 10k markets"
- Touched-market means: "~10/~30/~59 markets across the sweep" — "~85% of the engine window at 1,000 markets"
- Order footprint: "~321 B/order footprint spread over four structures crossing L2 → L3 → DRAM"
- Shock cells: "~73k ops/s with ~22.9 KB allocated per op" and "single sequenced price steps stalling the world for up to ~2.2 ms at only 10k accounts"
- Margin guard band: "±$1 guard band" (line 76)

Two footprint figures that differ and could matter to your review: R7 states target "~321 B/order toward ~200 B/order" (line 50), while the U4 hard test assertion is "≤ ~220 B" (line 178) and Definition of Done does not restate a footprint number at all (footprint is not in the DoD "Targets" bullet — only many-markets ≥1.4× and mild-shock ≤1,000 bytes/op are).

The file was read read-only; nothing was modified.

I've read the full 744-line log. Here's my evaluation.

## Verdict

The log is coherent and unusually honest. Both reverted levers are documented as tried-and-bounded with explicit reopening conditions (not assumed wins), regressions are disclosed rather than buried, it avoids opaque identifier schemes entirely, and it flags op-count-mix inflation with honest whole-run figures. I found **no blocking honesty defects**. A handful of advisory items below.

---

## 1. Host fingerprint, repro commands, final tables — all present

**Host fingerprint** (lines 5-16), repeated for the sliced-fanout series (524-536) and the series-final snapshot (647-657):
> `Host: yamen-superset-f4-med-syd-1` / `CPU: AMD EPYC 4564P 16-Core Processor` / `Build config: target_cpu=znver4 opt_level=3 lto=fat codegen_units=1 panic=unwind pgo=off` / `Rust: rustc 1.96.0 (ac68faa20 2026-05-25)` / `Pre-series engine baseline SHA from the playbook: a103a45`

**Repro commands**: baseline (22-33), each lever section carries its own (248-317, 376-411, 486-508, 598-641), and the final snapshot (663-688). All prefixed `env -u RUSTFLAGS`, pinned `taskset -c 2` for timing.

**Final benchmark tables**: "Standard grid at final HEAD" (694-704, "Median of three pinned full-grid repetitions. Every row reported `rejects=0`"), "Standard grid versus pre-series baseline" (712-722), and "Fan-out evidence at final HEAD" (735-738).

## 2. Per-lever outcomes and headline numbers

- **Shock allocation recycling — LANDED.** Line 130-134: "mild-shock improved from `4,799.413` to `408.978` bytes/op, roughly a twelve-fold reduction and below the 1,000 bytes/op target." Allocs "improved from `2.178` to `1.600` allocs/op for mild-shock, not an order-of-magnitude count reduction; bytes/op is the gated target."
- **Incremental Margin Aggregate — REVERTED net-negative** (155-182). Line 166-168: many-markets `0.88x median` throughput, p99.9 `0.72x to 0.79x`; mild-shock `0.840x median`, p99.9 `1.223x`; severe-cascade `0.667x median`, p99.9 `1.350x`. "the remediated build losing 14 or 15 of 15 paired rounds on every metric across both shock cells."
- **Order Record Unification — LANDED/validated at `2e4cfbc`** (184-321). "Per live order: `220.01` bytes... asserts the same footprint stays at or below `220.1` bytes per live order." Implementer many-markets throughput `1.203x` (214); independent validator `1.131x` (232); single-hot-book `0.999x` "flat within noise."
- **Batched maker-side effects — REVERTED measured-net-negative** (418-482). Validator severe-cascade worst price-batch `1.198x` (batched worse 8/10), throughput `0.940x`; mild-shock worst `1.100x` (9/10), throughput `0.929x`. "No shock cell showed a win."
- **Sliced Price-Step Fan-out — LANDED/validated at `42ab47b`** (510-594). Line 562-564: worst price-batch stall `118.406 ms → 3.653 ms` = `32.41x`; mean `103.104 ms → 0.231 ms` = `446.99x`. Validator (578-581): `34.2x` and `471x`. Final HEAD (742): `32.8x` and `437.7x`.
- **Fan-out evidence — validated, gate open** (323-416). Line 343: 100,000 holders unsplit worst `135.729 ms` vs split-four `34.974 ms` = `0.258x`, 3/3 paired split wins. Line 413-416: split "still leave tens of milliseconds at 100,000 holders, so they are supporting evidence rather than a substitute for resumable fan-out slicing."

## 3. Reverted levers documented as bounded with reopening conditions — yes

**Incremental Margin Aggregate** (179-182): "The lever is closed as measured net-negative and reverted. Reopen only with a design that avoids per-candidate staleness churn under mark-heavy load, only after the order-footprint lever lands because locality changes may shift the balance, and only with A/B evidence that meets the run's resolution rule on many-markets and both shock cells."

**Batched maker-side effects** (478-482): "Reopening condition: revisit deferred maker-side application only if the Sliced Price-Step Fan-out design needs it across slice boundaries or a future profile shows maker order-table writes or holder-index refreshes have become a distinct dominant stall again. Any reopening must re-derive the need from the current tree and pass its own order-flipped A/B on the direct shock price-batch stall metrics."

Both are framed as closed/reverted with the code recoverable (`1d72b5a`; `53b2654`), tree-equality proven — not as assumed wins.

## 4. Honesty check

**No opaque-identifier violations** — clean. Levers are named descriptively ("Sliced Price-Step Fan-out," "Order Record Unification"); modes are the real parameter values ("finite budget one" = `ENGINE_CORE_GRID_PRICE_HEALTH_SLICE_BUDGET=1", "split-four" = `SHOCK_SPLIT_BATCHES=4`); SHAs and env vars are exempt code references. No D1/F1/rung/Option-A codes.

**Regressions are disclosed, not buried** — strength. Line 724-727: "The final grid does not support a broad throughput-win claim." The final-vs-pre table (714-722) openly shows most standard-grid cells regressed: baseline `0.859x`, many-accounts `0.889x`, single-hot-book `0.922x`, many-markets `0.902x`, market-ceiling `0.869x`, mild-shock `0.902x` throughput.

**Cross-ruler discipline** — strength. Lines 121-134 explicitly refuse to compare the old ReportSink `18,468` warmed number against the counting-sink target, retaining it "as attribution evidence rather than as the target comparison."

Advisory items (none blocking):

- **[Advisory, lines 214 / 700 / 718] many-markets win vs final-grid regression use different workloads and could be conflated.** The order-unification A/B reports many-markets `1.203x`/`1.131x` at a `1,000,000 measured ops` window (old median 1,848,980 → new 2,225,065), but the standard-grid pre-series many-markets is `1,166,681` (line 87) and final HEAD is `1,052,308` = `0.902x` (line 718). Same cell name, two different "old" numbers and opposite directions depending on the measured-op window. The log discloses the window difference (217-219, 288-296) and narrows the accepted win to "the order unification series" (line 726), so it's hedged — but a reader must track which window applies.

- **[Advisory, lines 714-722] ~10-14% standard-grid throughput regressions are disclosed but unattributed and possibly host-confounded.** The final snapshot ran with an outside `hl_stream` workload live at nice 19 (656-657), while the pre-series baseline (16, 83-91) records only a clean checkout with no outside-load ruling. The log rightly labels this "a final snapshot, not an A/B claim" (708), but the baseline throughput drop is neither explained nor disentangled from measurement variance.

- **[Advisory, line 564] mean price-batch improvement ratio is slightly inflated by display rounding.** `103.104 / 0.231 = 446.3x`, but the log states `446.99x`. Similar small drifts: line 472 states `1.067x` where `0.183/0.172 = 1.064x`; final-HEAD `437.7x` (742) vs `112.009/0.256 = 437.5x`. All are sub-1% display-rounding artifacts from the two-to-three-decimal ms values, not internal contradictions — every ratio-of-medians I recomputed (32.41x, 34.2x, 471x, 1.198x, 0.940x, resident-storage 901,154 → 220.01 B/order, the 0.72–0.79x/12%/22%/35% margin-aggregate figures) reconciles.

## 5. Op-count-mix-inflated columns and honest whole-run figures — yes

Line 585-588: "The cell throughput column is op-count-mix inflated by those bench drain folds: the row shows `154k` to roughly `1.05M` ops/s because finite mode adds synthetic drain operations to the denominator. The honest whole-run figure is the perf elapsed ratio, `11.267579176s` to `8.385791033s`, about `1.34x`; cache-miss rate also drops from `18.51%` to `14.40%`."

Restated at final HEAD (740-742): "The throughput column is not the fan-out gate metric for this cell: finite mode adds drain folds, so the operation count mix changes. The direct stall columns are the honest comparison." The fan-out-evidence section makes the same point about whole-row percentiles (368-372): "changing the number of price batches changes the sample mix... The direct worst and mean price-batch columns isolate the stop-the-world stall and avoid that percentile contamination."

**Blocking:** none.

**Advisory:**

   vs `437.5x`). Every material ratio recomputes cleanly; these are two-to-three-decimal display
   artifacts, not internal inconsistencies.

### Bottom line

All eight acceptance criteria VERIFIED against recorded evidence and the tree. Both reverted levers
are clean tree-equal reverts with reopening conditions recorded; both landed levers
(Order Record Unification, Sliced Price-Step Fan-out) are present, guarded, and behavior-preserving
at their documented configs. The durable benchmark log stands on its own as an honest record — wins
and regressions both disclosed, op-count-mix inflation flagged with honest whole-run figures, no
opaque-identifier or chronological-narration violations. No blocking issues. The one item warranting

because the dominant lever was honestly reverted — recorded as a deliberate honest-miss, not a
